images.alt: Chinese city life intricately depicted inside an open white Chinese takeout box, transformed into a highly detailed miniature urban diorama. A bustling street scene runs through the center with tiny pedestrians crossing at striped crosswalks, small cars and buses moving along the road, and densely packed traditional-meets-modern Chinese buildings lining both sides. Architecture features tiled roofs, hanging red lanterns, vertical Chinese signage, and warmly glowing shop interiors. A small golden pavilion sits along the street adding a cultural focal point. The entire scene is captured with a tilt-shift effect, creating a realistic miniature illusion with shallow depth of field—sharp focus at the center and soft blur toward the edges. Lighting is warm and ambient, evoking early evening city life. The takeout box frames the composition, with chopsticks and a blurred dining table environment visible in the background. Photographic, candid, ultra-detailed, cinematic realism, square orientation.

My expression is a hollow, malevolent stare, looking just past the camera with a slack jaw and dead eyes. I am wearing a torn, dirty nightgown that is stained with earth and old blood. In the background, the wooden archway of the pet sematary is barely visible in the fog, and ancient, twisted trees loom overhead. The lighting is cold and blue, casting eerie, unnatural shadows.

My skin is corpse-pale and slick with sweat and a glowing neon green reagent that drips from my chin and splatters on the coat. My eyes are wide, manic, and dilated, with a hint of the green fluid reflecting in the pupils. My hair is disheveled, wet, and sticking to my face. In the background, shelves are lined with jars of organs and bubbling beakers, and a body is partially visible on a gurney under a harsh surgical light. The lighting is eerie, casting long shadows and emphasizing the fluorescent green glow of the liquid on my skin.
